hero.headline: Describe an app. Get a proof anyone can check.
hero.subhead: Infrix turns a plain-language prompt into a governed workflow, runs it, and produces a proof you can verify yourself — without trusting our node. Accumulate is the L0 that anchors it.
hero.promptPlaceholder: Describe the verifiable app you want…
examplePrompts: A regulated escrow with buyer approval, a regulator-only invoice view, and a shareable proof.
A release attestation signed by two maintainers that anyone can verify offline.
A grant payout that only releases after a milestone is approved, with a full audit trail.

primaryAction.label: Build verifiable app
primaryAction.command: infrix new verifiable-app my-escrow "a regulated escrow with buyer approval and a shareable proof"
primaryAction.why: Grounds the prompt, builds the workflow and SDK, runs it for a real proof, and verifies it — in one command.

receiptPreview.title: Regulated escrow — proof receipt
receiptPreview.plainSummary: An escrow released funds only after the buyer approved, a regulator could view the invoice, and two operators witnessed it. This receipt lets anyone re-check that outcome without trusting the Infrix node.
receiptPreview.assuranceLabel: Locally verified (L3 / G2). Live L0 not checked; the node is never trusted.
whatVerified: The governed workflow ran exactly as written (every step bound into the sealed story).
The buyer's approval was a real Ed25519 decision bound to the artifact hash.
The outcome replays deterministically from the sealed evidence.
The proof's integrity is recomputed offline — the node asserts nothing the verifier did not confirm.
whatNotVerified: The Accumulate L0 anchor (L4) — a local run never confirms it; run on Kermit for L0 confirmation.
The witness quorum (two independent operators) — verified on Kermit with registered witnesses, deferred on a local run.
receiptPreview.l0AnchorStatus: Not checked on this local run. On Kermit the anchor is confirmed against Accumulate L0 for L4.
receiptPreview.witnessStatus: Deferred: a Kermit run with registered witnesses proves the two-operator quorum.

cinemaPreview.title: Replay the run, step by step
cinemaPreview.note: Cinema is a non-committing view of the canonical artifacts — the L0-anchored spine is the record, never the movie.

sections.title: Verify without trusting the node
sections.body: The proof is yours to check. The verifier recomputes every binding offline and the Infrix node is never trusted for the outcome — if our node lied, your verification would fail.
points: Run the verifier on the bundle; it re-derives the result from sealed evidence.
Assurance always comes from the verifier (Source = verifykit), never from the node.
A local run proves L3 / G2; it is honest that the L0 anchor (L4) was not checked.

sections.title: Why Accumulate L0 matters
sections.body: Accumulate is the L0 that anchors a proof. When you run on Kermit, the proof's root is written to Accumulate L0, so the anchor is an on-chain fact (L4) — not something our node asserts.
points: The verifier confirms the anchor directly against Accumulate L0, bypassing the node.
L4 is reached only with a live Accumulate L0 anchor confirmation; local runs stay below it.
Tampering with the bundle breaks the anchor binding the verifier checks.

sections.title: Compared to a conventional smart-contract flow
sections.body: A conventional contract makes you trust a chain explorer and read bytecode. Infrix gives a plain-language outcome plus a portable proof. The comparison is measured in the demo, never asserted.
points: Conventional figures are sourced or labeled an assumption; Infrix figures are measured in the demo.
No claim that Infrix is "better" without a measured basis — the demo shows the numbers.

sections.title: For developers
sections.body: One command scaffolds, runs, and proves a verifiable app, and emits a typed SDK driver and an embeddable verification widget.
points: The generated SDK driver dry-runs and approves before it runs, and never targets mainnet.
The widget verifies the proof structure offline in the browser — locally, with no node trust.
sections.command: infrix new verifiable-app my-escrow "a regulated escrow with a shareable proof"

sections.title: For AI agents
sections.body: Agents drive the same governed actions through the Agent Action Protocol — every mutating action is dry-run and approval-gated, and assurance still comes from the verifier.
points: Point any MCP-capable agent at tools/mcp-infrix to expose the governed actions over stdio.
An agent can resume from the exact workspace state it left in .infrixapp/.

sections.title: Wallet & provider status
sections.body: MetaMask is supported through the SDK and API (the @infrix/client withMetaMask path) and covered by deterministic provider-simulation tests. A real-browser provider PASS is pending sealed acceptance evidence.
points: Connect, signature explanation, and key-page mapping are real and covered by tests.
The provider matrix stays honest: a PASS row requires a sealed real-browser acceptance bundle.

claimBoundary.kermit: On Kermit, a proof is anchored to Accumulate L0 and independently verifiable (up to L4 with a live anchor). Kermit is the official pre-mainnet proving network.
claimBoundary.mainnet: Mainnet is gated: a mainnet production claim requires referenced mainnet evidence we have not produced. Nothing here defaults to mainnet, and pilot invoices stay non-payable until a mainnet commercial mode exists.

boundaries.title: Kermit proof ≠ mainnet production claim
boundaries.weDoNotClaim: A Kermit proof is a mainnet production claim.
boundaries.honest: Kermit is the official pre-mainnet proving network. A Kermit proof is real and independently verifiable, but it is not a mainnet production claim — that requires referenced mainnet evidence we have not produced. Mainnet remains gated until that evidence exists.
boundaries.why: Verification on a testnet proves the mechanism, not production operation. We never let a Kermit result stand in for a mainnet guarantee.

boundaries.title: Local L3 proof ≠ L4
boundaries.weDoNotClaim: A local run produces an L4 proof.
boundaries.honest: A local run proves cryptographic structure and governance (L3 / G2). It is not L4: L4 requires confirming the anchor on Accumulate L0, which a local run never does. The verifier caps a local run below L4 and discloses what was deferred.
boundaries.why: L4 is an on-chain fact, not a local computation. Only a live Accumulate L0 anchor confirmation earns it, and the verifier — never the node — decides.

boundaries.title: MetaMask SDK support ≠ provider PASS
boundaries.weDoNotClaim: MetaMask is a finished, certified provider experience.
boundaries.honest: MetaMask is supported through the SDK and API (the @infrix/client withMetaMask path) and covered by deterministic provider-simulation tests. That is not a provider PASS: a PASS requires a sealed real-browser acceptance bundle (tools/metamask-acceptance and the provider matrix), which stays pending until a real Chrome + MetaMask run is captured.
boundaries.why: Provider-simulation tests prove the logic; only a captured real-browser pass proves the user-facing flow. We keep the provider matrix honest about which is which.

boundaries.title: On-chain token & staking are gated
boundaries.weDoNotClaim: The on-chain INFRIX token and staking are operating today.
boundaries.honest: The off-chain IU billing meter is live and metered from real verifier verdicts. The on-chain INFRIX token and staking remain gated on pilot demand and stay planned until they ship on-chain.
boundaries.why: Shipping a token before there is pilot demand would be theater. The economics layer is real; the on-chain instrument waits for a reason to exist.

boundaries.title: Commercial pilot invoices are non-payable
boundaries.weDoNotClaim: Pilot invoices can be settled today.
boundaries.honest: Commercial pilot invoices are generated from the IU meter, but they are non-payable: settlement requires a mainnet commercial mode that does not yet exist. Until then, invoices are informational and frame the eventual commercial relationship.
boundaries.why: We will not present a payable invoice for a settlement path that is not built. The meter is real; payment is gated on the commercial mode landing.
